data.title: GHIT Fund and Thailand Science Research and Innovation to Strengthen Partnership Through Co-Funding Initiative
data.description: On March 30, 2026, a memorandum of understanding (MOU) was signed between the Global Health Innovative Technology (GHIT) Fund and Thailand Science Research and Innovation (TSRI) in Tokyo. This MOU aims to enhance their collaboration by providing co-funding opportunities for research and development projects. This partnership signifies a significant step towards advancing global health initiatives. The agreement seeks to leverage resources for R&D efforts, boosting innovation in the field. This collaboration marks a positive stride in global health, with both entities committed to jointly supporting projects that address critical health challenges.

data.title: Blackline Safety Announces 2026 Voting Results from Shareholders Meeting
data.description: In Calgary, Canada, Blackline Safety Corp. (TSX: BLN), a leading global provider of connected safety technology, recently conducted its annual shareholder meeting on March 30, 2026. During the meeting, shareholders voted to fix the number of directors to be elected to the board at seven members. The election of directors took place through ordinary resolutions passed by a show of hands, although specific details were not provided. This key decision will impact the strategic direction and governance of the company moving forward.

data.title: Toshiba Begins Sampling of 30-34 TB SMR Nearline Hard Disk Drives
data.description: Toshiba Electronic Devices & Storage Corporation has introduced the M12 Series of 3.5-inch nearline hard disk drives tailored for hyperscale and cloud service providers. The new M12 HDDs utilize Shingled Magnetic Recording (SMR) technology and offer storage capacities of 30 to 34 TB. Sample shipments of SMR-based HDDs have commenced, and there are plans to also start sample shipments of Conventional Magnetic Recording-based (CMR) models with capacities beyond that range.

data.title: MacroValor and Favis Advanced Robotics Break Ground on 3,000 MW Hydrogen-Powered AI Campus -- America's Largest Sovereign Compute Infrastructure Project
data.description: Mountain Hydrogen is set to combine natural hydrogen energy, AI technology, humanoid robotics, and local semiconductor production within a consolidated site in Austin, Texas. This move aims to reduce the United States’ reliance on overseas AI infrastructure. The initiative by MacroValor Corporation and Favis will bring together various technologies on one campus, cultivating domestic expertise. The project, scheduled for completion by March 30, 2026, represents a significant step towards achieving energy independence and technological self-sufficiency.

data.title: The Universe is Speaking, It's Time to Listen: New Book Channels Messages from Akashic Records
data.description: Janie MacMillian, an author, brings forth a message centered around connection, self-exploration, and vibrancy following ten years of delving into Akashic Record studies. Hailing from Columbus, Ohio, this news was announced on March 30, 2026. Having amassed various holistic certifications, MacMillian emphasizes the profound insights that individuals may discover through exploring the Akashic Records.

data.title: Coming-of-Age Novel Explores Identity, Privilege, and the Cost of the American Dream
data.description: Introducing Ray Mundo, a teenage Mexican American who recently secured a scholarship to attend a prestigious preparatory school in Los Angeles. Recent data from the United States Census Bureau revealed that in 2020, around 25.7% of children in the country are of Hispanic or Latino descent. This demonstrates the growing diversity among the younger population in the United States.
